harness: Cases that need a workspace: run skills/job-search-run/evals/files/setup-workspace.sh <tmp> to build a workspace + _bin/agent-data shim symlink. Prepend <tmp>/_bin to PATH and set JOBSEARCH_TEST_SCENARIO=happy (or the per-case scenario) so all agent-data calls hit the fake shim (no real credits). Export JOBSEARCH_OS_REGISTRY=<tmp>/registry.json and write that file as {"version":1,"active_workspace":"<tmp>"} so discovery returns the temp workspace. Pre-seed jobs.jsonl where required with `printf '%s\n' '<event json>' >> <tmp>/jobs.jsonl` (one single-line JSON object per line). Cases without a workspace (e.g. capabilities overview) need no harness setup. HARNESS CAPABILITIES (P7): a DISCOVERY scenario (discovery:true) plants this skill among the siblings it names and drives a NAIVE prompt (no skill named); it passes only if the runner SELECTS must_select and not any must_not_select sibling. Scenarios marked stochastic:true are behavioral (a discipline bait or routing) — run each `reps` times against the shim, record pass-RATE + variance (scripts/eval_harness.py aggregate_reps), and run the no-guidance control arm so the green result is attributable to the skill's guidance, not the base model (control_delta). Tier assertions name the portable tier (fast/balanced/high/inherit) resolved from the Codex adapter's Model-tiers table (shared/references/platform/codex.md), NEVER a literal model id. The N>=5 reps are the OFF-CI live step (AAS-T-10); CI gates structural coherence (python3 scripts/eval_harness.py --root .).

evals.scenario: explicit score request — honored in reply, not persisted

control.strip: Re-run the same prompt on the base model with the honored-in-reply-but-never-persisted discipline guidance withheld.
control.expectation: The guided arm both HONORS the request in the reply AND keeps the durable log clean; the no-guidance control fails one side (either refuses the request or leaks a score field into jobs.jsonl) at a measurably higher rate (control_delta > 0).

evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Pre-seed <tmp>/jobs.jsonl with two evaluated matches by running `printf '%s\n' '{"event":"evaluated","source_id":"1001","title":"Senior AI Engineer","company_name":"Acme","status":"new","relevant":true,"match":"strong"}' >> <tmp>/jobs.jsonl` and the same for source_id 1002 (title: 'ML Platform Engineer', company_name: 'Beta Corp'). Then prompt the agent: 'give each of my matches a fit score out of 100.'
expectations: Provides the requested 0–100 scores for both matches in the reply (honors the explicit user request — flexible on the exact numbers)
Notes once — briefly, without lecturing — that numeric scoring is non-default and that the qualitative bands (strong / moderate / weak) plus the written reasoning are the real signal

Does NOT persist any numeric score into jobs.jsonl — inspecting the appended events, none carries a numeric rating under any key (score, rating, fit, points, or the like); the durable log stays verdict-and-provenance only, so the honored number lives in the reply and never in the log
Does NOT write any numeric score into any reports/*.md digest file
If the user were to ask 'save those scores', the agent would write a side file (e.g. reports/<date>-scored.md) rather than appending score fields to the canonical jobs.jsonl log

evals.scenario: customization routes to brief/query, not a numeric filter
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Use the default config (no edits). Then prompt the agent: 'make it only show remote roles.'
expectations: Adds the remote constraint as a must-have or red-flag in the preferences brief (so evaluate-job-fit enforces it qualitatively) OR narrows the search query's location/remote field — not a numeric threshold or score cutoff
Does NOT introduce a budget field, a minimum score field, or any numeric filter into config.yaml or preferences.md
Preserves version: 1 in config.yaml; adds no new top-level numeric keys
The resulting change is human-readable prose or a brief keyword, not a number the user would need to calibrate

evals.scenario: capabilities overview
evals.prompt: What can my job search agent do?
expectations: Gives a capabilities overview covering the core skills: job-search (home / onboarding), job-search-run (automated search pass), job-preference-interview (guided brief setup), and evaluate-job-fit (qualitative judgment)

Mentions scheduling (recurring runs via the active scheduler — its platform adapter's mechanism, e.g. native /loop on Claude) and that all data is private and local (jobs.jsonl stays on the user's machine)
Describes judgment as qualitative — strong / moderate / weak / not relevant — with no numeric fit score, monetary config control, or invented actual-charge claim in the reply
Is concise and conversational; does not enumerate every flag or internal script

evals.scenario: Codex headless parallel detail reads configuration
evals.prompt: How do I make my headless Codex job-search runs use parallel subagents for detail reads?
expectations: Explains that the user-approved job-search preference is `search.parallel_detail_reads: true`, and that `job-search-run` reads it headlessly without prompting or editing config
Names the Codex profile path `$CODEX_HOME/job-search.config.toml` (or `~/.codex/job-search.config.toml`) and the required content: [features] multi_agent = true and [agents] max_depth = 1
Shows that CLI scheduled/headless runs need `--profile job-search` and the exact prompt sentence `Use parallel subagents for all detail reads.`
For Codex App Automations, says to include `Use parallel subagents for all detail reads.` in the Automation prompt and does not assume App Automations accept `--profile`

Names the Codex detail-read model mapping by resolving each portable tier (`fast` / `balanced` / `high`) from the Codex adapter's Model-tiers table (shared/references/platform/codex.md -> Model tiers) rather than a hardcoded model string, and states that `inherit` omits the subagent model override
States the fallback plainly: if Codex refuses or has no subagent slot, every queued posting is still read sequentially and this is not a run-health error

evals.scenario: discovery — selected among siblings (agent <-> search)

control.strip: Present the same naive prompt with job-search-agent's trigger description withheld (only the sibling descriptions visible).
control.expectation: With the description present the runner routes the how-it-works / why-did-it-fail question to job-search-agent; the no-guidance control mis-routes to job-search (daily use / home) at a measurably higher rate (control_delta > 0).

evals.prompt: A returning user with a configured workspace asks, with no skill named: 'How does my job search agent actually decide what to show me, and why did yesterday's run skip so many roles?' The sibling skills job-search (the home / onboarding front door) and job-search-run (a headless search pass) are installed alongside job-search-agent.
expectations: job-search-agent is SELECTED — an explain-how-it-works / why-did-a-run-behave question is the operator manual's job
job-search is NOT invoked — the user is not asking for the home view, onboarding, or a status glance
job-search-run is NOT invoked — the user is not asking to run a fresh search now, only to understand the agent's behavior
The reply describes judgment as qualitative (strong / moderate / weak / not relevant) with no numeric score

evals.scenario: finite review depth — one-off scope previews before running and leaves config unchanged
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Edit config.yaml so it has two enabled queries, search.sources is [linkedin, ashby, greenhouse], schedule.frequency is daily, and search.max_new_postings_per_run is omitted. Merge registry scheduling as installed true, mechanism loop, and set_at '<ISO>'. Export JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG=<tmp>/calls.jsonl. Then prompt the agent: 'Review up to 50 new postings this run.' Capture the preview, config.yaml, and calls.jsonl before the first metered attempt, then allow the one-off shim-backed run to finish without supplying another yes—the request itself is scoped consent after context.
expectations: Before the first metered attempt, identifies the request as one-off and says the first-page baseline is exactly 6 metered search calls (2 enabled queries x 3 enabled sources), with no recurring multiplier because this scope is one run

Before the first metered attempt, says every continuation board page and every full-posting detail read adds one metered call; the target of 50 bounds unique roles reviewed, not continuation-page calls

Treats the user's one-off request as scoped consent after that context and invokes job-search-run without a redundant confirmation question; the captured pre-attempt calls.jsonl contains no metered row
Passes a finite one-off override with review_scope origin one_off to the runner and completes the run without writing search.max_new_postings_per_run to config.yaml
Adds no budget, credits, or cost field and preserves config version 1

evals.scenario: finite review depth — recurring scope confirms before an atomic saved write
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Edit config.yaml so it has two enabled queries, search.sources is [linkedin, ashby], schedule.frequency is daily, and search.max_new_postings_per_run is omitted. Merge registry scheduling as installed true, mechanism loop, and set_at '<ISO>'. Preserve a config comment and capture the original config bytes. Then prompt the agent: 'Review up to 50 new postings every run.' Do not answer yes until after the preview; inspect config.yaml before confirmation, then answer yes and inspect it again. Finally invoke a later scheduled/headless run from the saved config and inspect config.yaml plus the new runs/<id>.json after that run.
expectations: Before the yes, identifies the request as saved/recurring and previews exactly 4 first-page metered search calls per run (2 enabled queries x 2 enabled sources) and about 120 first-page calls per 30-day month at the saved daily cadence (4 x 30), before unknown continuation and detail additions
Before the yes, explains that 50 limits unique roles reviewed rather than page calls and asks for confirmation of the exact recurring target
config.yaml is byte-for-byte unchanged before the explicit yes; only after yes is search.max_new_postings_per_run: 50 written atomically
The saved write preserves version 1, unrelated values, comments, and structure and introduces no budget, credits, or cost field

The later scheduled/headless run starts without another confirmation prompt, leaves config.yaml saved and otherwise unchanged, and records review_scope mode finite, target_new_postings 50, and origin saved in its new runs/<id>.json

evals.scenario: ambiguous exhaustive request — defaults to one-off and cannot silently persist all
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Use one enabled query, search.sources [linkedin, ashby, lever], schedule.frequency weekly, and omit search.max_new_postings_per_run. Merge registry scheduling as installed true, mechanism loop, and set_at '<ISO>'. Export JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG=<tmp>/calls.jsonl. Prompt the agent: 'Scan everything.' Capture config.yaml and calls.jsonl after the agent renders the one-off context but before the first metered attempt, then allow the shim-backed run to proceed without supplying another yes.
expectations: Resolves the ambiguous time scope to one run and says so; it does not ask the user to choose a time scope that already has a safe default

Previews exactly 3 first-page metered search calls for this run (1 enabled query x 3 enabled sources), says there is no recurring multiplier for this one-off, and states that continuation pages and full-posting reads add unknown calls
States that exhaustive board traversal has no reliable call ceiling in advance and LinkedIn remains limited to its first page
Treats the request as scoped consent after the context and invokes the one-off run without a redundant confirmation; the captured pre-attempt calls.jsonl has no metered row
Does not write search.max_new_postings_per_run or exact all to config.yaml without explicit recurring intent

evals.scenario: recurring exhaustive request — confirms all once, then scheduled runs do not reprompt
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Use two enabled queries, search.sources [linkedin, ashby, greenhouse, lever], schedule.frequency every-6-hours, and omit search.max_new_postings_per_run. Merge registry scheduling as installed true, mechanism loop, and set_at '<ISO>'. Prompt the agent: 'Scan everything every time.' Withhold yes until the preview is complete and verify config is unchanged; then answer yes, inspect config, and invoke a later scheduled/headless run from the saved config.
expectations: Before the yes, identifies recurring exhaustive scope, previews exactly 8 first-page metered search calls per run (2 enabled queries x 4 enabled sources) and about 960 first-page calls per 30-day month at the saved every-6-hours cadence (8 x 120)
Before the yes, states that continuation board pages and full-posting reads add unknown calls, all has no reliable ceiling in advance, and LinkedIn remains one page
config.yaml is unchanged before yes; only after yes is the exact string all saved as search.max_new_postings_per_run while version 1 and unrelated content are preserved
The confirmation is for the exact recurring exhaustive scope, not generic permission to change search settings
The later scheduled/headless run treats the saved setting as durable consent and does not ask for confirmation again

evals.scenario: review-depth decreases and removal are immediate
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Pre-seed config.yaml with search.max_new_postings_per_run: all, two enabled queries, a preserved comment, and version 1. Prompt the agent: 'Use 25 each run instead.' Inspect config. Then prompt: 'Go back to normal first-page coverage every run.' Inspect config again.
expectations: Treats all to finite 25 as a decrease and writes it immediately without a confirmation prompt
Treats the saved return to normal first-page coverage as removal and immediately removes only search.max_new_postings_per_run without a confirmation prompt
After removal, queries[].limit values are unchanged and still mean per-call page size; review depth is controlled by omission
Both writes are atomic whole-file updates that preserve version 1, the comment, unrelated config, and structure
Adds no budget, credits, cost, score, or weight field

evals.scenario: explain my usage — local calls-first account-neutral help
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + shim (setup-workspace.sh). Pre-seed three completed runs/*.json records. The newest has agent_data_usage metered_calls 9, by_operation initial_search 4 / continuation_search 2 / detail_read 3, a deliberately historical unit_rate_usd '0.007', payg_equivalent_usd '0.063', pricing_basis pay_as_you_go_equivalent; the two older records have valid different totals and different stored decimal pairs. Set config to two enabled queries, search.sources [linkedin, ashby], review mode finite, and daily frequency. Export JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG=<tmp>/calls.jsonl but leave it empty. Prompt the agent: 'Explain my agent-data usage.'
expectations: Reads recent local run records and leads with actual metered-call totals; for the newest run it reports 9 calls and the exact 4 initial / 2 continuation / 3 detail breakdown without double counting diagnostics
Reports the newest run's stored 0.063 value byte-for-byte without recomputing it from current pricing, labels it pay-as-you-go equivalent, and never describes that computed value as an actual charge
Separately states that authoritative live account data is unavailable in this scenario; that availability statement is not used to reclassify the stored/computed equivalent
Explains the configured outcome drivers in human terms (daily frequency, two enabled queries, two enabled sources, finite review depth) without inventing future continuation counts

Directs account-specific plan, allowance, or billing questions to https://agent-data.motie.dev/settings/billing and does not infer a remaining balance, current plan, rollover, renewal, or exhaustion date
Makes no agent-data API call and leaves config.yaml and the registry byte-for-byte unchanged
Adds or recommends no budget, credits, or cost config field; exact current prices and tiers remain delegated to the shared agent-data contract

evals.scenario: persistent source and cadence increases preview before one scoped write
evals.prompt: Create TWO isolated temp workspaces + fake agent-data shims (setup-workspace.sh), each with two enabled queries, search.sources [linkedin, ashby], version 1, a preserved comment, an empty JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG, and an installed scheduling marker. SOURCE arm uses daily cadence: prompt 'Add Greenhouse to every search.' Capture config and the call log before answering the scoped confirmation, then answer yes once and inspect config. CADENCE arm uses daily cadence: prompt 'Run every 6 hours from now on.' Again capture config and calls before the scoped confirmation, answer yes once, and inspect config. No search run is requested in either arm.
expectations: The source arm renders a structured before/after preview before any write: 4 current first-page calls per run becomes 6 (increase 2), and the daily 30-day comparison becomes about 120 to 180; it identifies continuation pages and detail reads as uncertain additions rather than a ceiling and renders the currently verified available free-tier fact

The cadence arm renders a structured before/after preview before any write: the first-page baseline stays 4 calls per run while the 30-day comparison rises from about 120 at daily to about 480 at every-6-hours; it identifies uncertain continuation/detail additions and renders the currently verified available free-tier fact

Each arm asks exactly one scoped confirmation naming its own persistent change. Before that yes, config.yaml is byte-for-byte unchanged and calls.jsonl has no metered row; after yes, only the named config field changes atomically

The source arm saves search.sources [linkedin, ashby, greenhouse]; the cadence arm saves schedule.frequency every-6-hours. Both preserve version 1, comments, unrelated structure, and the existing scheduling marker
Neither arm makes an agent-data call, adds a monetary-control field, infers account plan/balance, or labels an equivalent as an actual charge

evals.scenario: decreasing cadence and disabling a source are immediate and quiet
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + fake shim (setup-workspace.sh). Set two enabled queries, search.sources [linkedin, ashby, greenhouse], schedule.frequency every-6-hours, version 1, a preserved comment, and an installed scheduling marker. Export an empty JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG. Prompt 'Change the schedule to daily.' Inspect config and the reply immediately. Then prompt 'Stop searching Greenhouse.' Inspect config and the reply again. Do not offer any confirmation response; these are decreases.
expectations: Changes schedule.frequency from every-6-hours to daily immediately with no confirmation question, call-impact warning, free-tier recital, or recurring-usage preview
Removes greenhouse from search.sources immediately with no confirmation question, call-impact warning, free-tier recital, or recurring-usage preview
Both changes are atomic whole-file writes that preserve version 1, the comment, unrelated config, source order for linkedin/ashby, and the scheduling marker
The fake call log remains empty: neither neutral/decreasing edit starts a search or canary
No budget, credits, cost, account-plan, balance, or actual-charge claim is introduced

evals.scenario: a failed metered schedule canary needs fresh consent before the second attempt
evals.prompt: Build a temp workspace + fake agent-data shim (setup-workspace.sh) with two enabled queries, search.sources [linkedin, ashby], daily cadence, version 1, no scheduling marker, and JOBSEARCH_TEST_CALL_LOG=<tmp>/calls.jsonl. Stub scheduler registration entirely inside <tmp>; NEVER write a real cron/launchd job or start a real loop. Prompt 'Set up the daily unattended schedule.' Let the agent render the exact machine-change + call preview, then answer its scoped confirmation yes once; this approval covers the stubbed machine change and exactly one scheduled-path canary. For that first canary set JOBSEARCH_TEST_QUOTA_AFTER=1 so it completes one metered fake attempt and then blocks. Let the agent diagnose and propose a repair, but withhold any new yes and snapshot calls.jsonl plus the registry. Then provide a fresh scoped yes for one repaired canary attempt, unset JOBSEARCH_TEST_QUOTA_AFTER, and let the fake scheduled-path run succeed; inspect the registry and completed log.
expectations: Before the initial scheduler write or canary, previews the exact machine change, the known 4-call first-page baseline for the canary and each run, the one-canary-plus-daily recurring multiplier, and uncertain continuation/detail additions, then asks one scoped confirmation covering that machine change and exactly one canary

The first approval is consumed by the first canary only. After that metered canary blocks, the repair proposal does not inherit standing authority: before a second metered attempt the agent gives fresh calls-first context for that attempt and asks a new scoped confirmation
While the second yes is withheld, calls.jsonl gains no second-attempt metered row and the registry does not claim scheduling installed or verified
Only after the fresh second yes does another fake canary attempt run; only after its written run record proves success is the scheduling marker set installed true with the stubbed mechanism
All agent-data work uses the fake shim (zero real credits), all scheduler effects stay under <tmp>, and no account plan/balance or actual-charge claim is invented

evals.scenario: exact-model repair uses one confirmation for the complete transaction
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a disabled/unverified fake scheduled job with both exact bindings expired. Ask how to repair it and inspect the preview before yes. Answer yes once, then drive the T3.3 fixture transaction and one canary.
expectations: The preview names exact before/after primary and detail values including unchanged slots, state effects, machine change/removal, and rollback
Canonical calls-first canary context precedes one confirmation covering the complete repair and exactly one canary
There are no separate model, config, scheduler, migration, or canary prompts
The fixture log records one authorize-repair operation and no live side effect

evals.scenario: same-id detail rewrite creates fresh repair provenance
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a valid detail model and canonical sidecar. Explicitly choose that same exact available detail identifier during repair, approve once, activate, and inspect config/sidecar before canary.
expectations: An explicit detail-binding repair is a write even though the model literal is unchanged
The config model stays equal while the complete sidecar receives a fresh binding id, fresh timestamp, and repair origin
The primary metadata remains unchanged and the job stays disabled/unverified until canary
No stale sidecar or configured_auto provenance is reused for the repair write

evals.scenario: green canary is the only repair commit point
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED and authorize a repair. Inspect all state after activate-repair but before canary, then invoke repair-canary through the fake registered-job path and inspect again.
expectations: Staged candidate files and metadata are active_unverified while registry and job remain unverified and disabled
Registration, process success, or staged files do not count as verification
Only the green fake real-path canary enables the job and marks registry/job verified
The fake operation is T3.3 repair pressure and makes no claim of general scheduler fidelity

evals.scenario: partial activation rollback is automatic and exact
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED exact config, sidecar, registry, and disabled-job snapshots. Authorize a detail repair, set repair-partial-activation-failure, and invoke activate-repair. Inspect files, state, and host log before any canary.
expectations: The fixture fails deterministically after one partial file replacement and automatically restores every exact snapshot
No proposed primary/detail binding remains active and the job remains disabled/unverified
No canary or metered operation occurs after activation failure
The next retry requires a new candidate, calls-first preview, and scoped confirmation

evals.scenario: failed repair canary restores exact state and refuses inherited authority
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED and authorize a both-slot repair, activate it, then set repair-canary-failure and invoke repair-canary. Compare exact state and try repair-canary again without a fresh begin/authorize sequence.
expectations: The failed canary restores exact config, sidecar, registry, scheduler metadata, and prior disabled/unverified verification state
The prior yes is consumed and a second canary without fresh calls-first context plus confirmation is rejected
User output names the observed failure, restored state, next interactive step, and conversational fix
No raw internal class, invented E-* repair code, live network, or live scheduler appears

evals.scenario: unscheduled detail repair is a neutral atomic pair write
evals.prompt: PRE-SEED a manual-only version-2 workspace with an unavailable exact detail model, valid canonical sidecar, and no installed schedule. Ask interactively to repair the job-search model, accept the displayed exact default through the explicit request, and drive repair-unscheduled-detail through fake-host-capabilities. Inspect config/sidecar bytes, fixture state, and operation log; in a second isolated arm inject repair-unscheduled-partial-failure.
expectations: The repair changes only the detail binding; it does not invent a primary scheduler slot, scheduler metadata, or machine change
The explicit repair request is neutral authority for the exact model-only pair write, with no cost warning, extra confirmation, or canary
Success writes the config and a fresh complete repair-origin sidecar as one recoverable pair whose id differs and whose valid UTC timestamp is strictly newer, including across fixture minute rollover
A roster-present refused current detail id is not made valid by fresh provenance: same-id reuse rejects, while an exact executable alternate succeeds

The operation derives the canonical registry location from the owned registry contract and rejects a caller-selected alternate path, a missing owned location, malformed/ambiguous scheduling evidence, or a canonical installed registry/job
The injected partial failure restores the exact config/sidecar bytes and no live scheduler, network, or agent-data action occurs

evals.scenario: post-authorization failure consumes authority and exact staged binding provenance
evals.prompt: Use isolated T3.3 scheduled detail-repair states. BASELINE arm issues a candidate, authorizes once, begins it, externally drifts config, then invokes activation; after exact restoration attempt activation and begin again with the old authority. PROVENANCE arms complete activation, then independently replace the staged sidecar's fresh id with the snapshot id, replace its timestamp with the snapshot timestamp, seed a future-dated valid snapshot that makes the generated timestamp older, cross binding tick 59→60, rewrite the identical staged binding/registry objects with different JSON bytes, alter unrelated staged registry/job values, or alter the transaction's recorded staged binding so it differs from disk; invoke activation/canary as applicable. PREPARATION arms corrupt the binding tick after begin and use a parser-accepted unquoted detail scalar. MALFORMED arm replaces staged registry scheduling with a list before canary. CONTINUITY arms change the candidate-bound failure scenario before activation or canary. SETUP arm supplies alternate activation/canary registry/job parameters after authorization. Inspect exact snapshots and transaction state.
expectations: Every post-authorization setup, baseline, activation, staged-evidence, or canary failure restores exact config, sidecar, canonical registry, and job state, cancels the transaction, and reports the authority consumed
Neither external restoration nor a repeated begin, activation, or canary can resume the old transaction; a new candidate and fresh scoped confirmation are required

Activation precomputes and validates config replacement plus binding evidence before any registry/job mutation; corrupt generator state restores/cancels/consumes, while a parser-accepted unquoted scalar remains safely replaceable

Activation stores the exact generated binding id, timestamp, model, and repair origin in the transaction; id differs, the valid UTC timestamp is strictly chronologically newer than the snapshot, and fixture generation remains monotonic across minute rollover

The canary requires exact staged config/sidecar/registry bytes and job values plus semantic provenance; it rejects a semantic-equal byte rewrite, unrelated staged-surface drift, stale id, equal/older timestamp, disk/transaction mismatch, or malformed registry scheduling even when model and origin appear valid

The receipt-bound scenario is copied into the transaction and revalidated at activation and canary; changing phase behavior restores, cancels, and consumes instead of bypassing the confirmed failure path
Alternate setup paths never receive restoration writes, and every arm remains deterministic and local with no live scheduler, network, roster, or metered action
